sections.paragraph:
Original: סלכה

Transliteration: salkâh

Phonetic: sal-kaw'

BDB Definition:

Salcah or Salchah = "migration"
  1. a town or district at the extreme eastern limit of Bashan and allocated to the tribe of Gad; modern 'Sulkhad' which is 56 miles

Origin: from an unused root meaning to walk

TWOT entry: None

Part(s) of speech: Proper Name Location
